title: Inventory Shrinkage Adjustment
event: Reyes Co.'s perpetual records show Merchandise Inventory of $21,000 at year-end, but a physical count finds $20,250 on hand. Prepare the adjusting entry.

lines.label: 21,000 − 20,250
lines.why: Lost, damaged, or stolen goods become part of COGS — the standard home for shrinkage.
lines.trap: Not a separate 'theft loss' line for normal amounts — shrinkage folds into COGS.

lines.label: down to count
lines.why: Brings the perpetual record down to what physically exists.
lines.trap: The COUNT wins. Perpetual records are a running estimate; the count is reality.

memorize.body: Perpetual systems still require a physical count — shrinkage is why. Records tell you what SHOULD be there; the count tells you what IS.
Adjust BY the difference (750), landing the account AT the counted 20,250 — same by/to pattern as supplies.

questions.prompt: Records show $21,000, count shows $20,250. Shrinkage adjustment amount = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the counted balance the account should land ON. The entry records only the 750 difference.
The count disagrees with the records, so an adjustment is required — the count always wins.

build.scaffold: Records minus count = the entry amount. Debit COGS, credit Inventory.
